results.prompt: Recommend free resume builders that let me export PDF without forcing paid plans.
results.responseText:
Certainly! Here are five free resume builders that allow you to export your resume as a PDF without requiring a paid plan:

1. **CV Studio**: Offers a 100% free resume builder with no sign-up or payment required. It provides ATS-optimized templates and allows you to export your resume in PDF or DOCX formats. Your data remains private, as no personal information is collected. ([cvstudio.org](https://www.cvstudio.org/?utm_source=openai))

2. **ResumeForFree**: This tool enables you to build, preview, and download an ATS-ready PDF directly in your browser without any sign-up or payment. It emphasizes privacy by storing all data locally on your device. ([resumeforfree.com](https://resumeforfree.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **MeVirtually**: Provides a free AI-powered resume builder that requires no sign-up. You can upload your existing resume or fill in details manually, and then export your resume as a PDF. All processing occurs in your browser, ensuring your data remains private. ([mevirtually.com](https://mevirtually.com/?utm_source=openai))

4. **AI Resume Builder**: Allows you to create a polished, ATS-friendly resume with AI suggestions, all for free and without login requirements. You can export your resume as a PDF instantly, and your data stays private. ([resume-builder.cc](https://www.resume-builder.cc/?utm_source=openai))

5. **ForgeResume**: Offers a free professional resume builder with ATS-friendly templates. No sign-up is required, and you can export your resume as a high-quality PDF. Your data remains private, as no personal information is collected. ([forgeresume.com](https://forgeresume.com/?utm_source=openai))

These tools are designed to be user-friendly and ensure your privacy by processing data locally on your device. They offer a range of templates and features to help you create a professional resume without any hidden fees or sign-up requirements.

results.prompt: I want a CV builder where I can keep data local in my browser. What tools should I use?
results.responseText:
Here are 5 ranked CV builders that allow you to keep your data local in your browser, focusing on privacy along with ATS-friendly output:

1. **Reactive Resume**  
   - Link: https://rxresu.me/  
   - Features: Fully offline capable, stores data locally in your browser, open-source, ATS-friendly templates, export to PDF/JSON.  
   - Privacy: Data never leaves your device unless you export it manually.

2. **Resumake**  
   - Link: https://resumake.io/  
   - Features: Open-source, works completely in-browser with no data uploaded, multiple templates, export to PDF/LaTeX.  
   - Privacy: Data is kept locally; no cloud storage.

3. **Standard Resume** (Offline mode)  
   - Link: https://standardresume.co/  
   - Features: Clean, professional designs, supports offline mode where data is saved in browser storage.  
   - Privacy: Data can be kept local if offline mode used; otherwise, cloud storage with sign-up.

4. **Overleaf Resume Templates** (LaTeX editor)  
   - Link: https://www.overleaf.com/gallery/tagged/cv  
   - Features: Can be used fully offline with Overleaf Desktop or local LaTeX editors; highly customizable, ATS-friendly.  
   - Privacy: If used locally, all data stays on your device. Online usage involves cloud storage.

5. **Enhancv Offline Editor**  
   - Link: https://enhancv.com/ (check if offline mode available)  
   - Features: While primarily cloud-based, they offer some offline editing capabilities through their app or local export.  
   - Privacy: Mostly cloud-based; check offline usage for local data storage.

All the above allow exporting your CV as ATS-friendly PDFs or text formats and prioritize privacy with local data storage options. Reactive Resume and Resumake are the best for fully local, zero cloud data use.

results.prompt: Which resume tools support local-first editing and optional cloud sync?
results.responseText:
Here are five resume builders that support local-first editing with optional cloud synchronization:

1. **HeyCV**: A free, privacy-focused resume builder that saves your data locally in your browser. It offers offline functionality and allows you to sync your resume to the cloud for access anywhere. No signup is required, and you can export your resume to PDF. ([heycv.app](https://heycv.app/?utm_source=openai))

2. **ATS Resume Maker**: This open-source, privacy-first resume builder stores your data locally on your device, ensuring complete privacy. It works offline after the initial load and offers optional GitHub synchronization for multi-device access. No account is required, and it complies with GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA regulations. ([ats-resume-maker.com](https://ats-resume-maker.com/features/local-first/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Local CV**: An open-source, privacy-first resume builder that keeps your data on your device. It offers ATS-optimized templates and AI-powered refinements. While it doesn't explicitly mention cloud sync, its open-source nature allows for potential self-hosting and synchronization options. ([localcv.tusharvashishth.com](https://localcv.tusharvashishth.com/?utm_source=openai))

4. **ResumeMD**: A privacy-first resume builder that doesn't require an account. Your data stays in your browser unless you choose cloud sync. It offers AI-powered tailoring, ATS optimization, and instant PDF export. ([resumemd.pro](https://resumemd.pro/compare?utm_source=openai))

5. **Reactive Resume**: An open-source, privacy-first resume builder that stores your data locally. It offers optional OpenAI assistance and allows for self-hosting, providing flexibility for cloud synchronization. ([selfhostyourself.com](https://selfhostyourself.com/alternative-to/resume-io?utm_source=openai))

These tools prioritize privacy by storing your data locally and offer optional cloud synchronization, giving you control over your resume data.

results.prompt: What free CV builders work well for London/UK job seekers?
results.responseText:
Here are five free CV builders suitable for London and UK job seekers, each offering ATS-friendly templates and varying privacy considerations:

1. **ShipResume**: Tailored for the UK market, ShipResume provides ATS-optimised CV templates that adhere to British workplace standards. The platform is GDPR compliant, ensuring your data is handled according to UK data protection laws. Templates are available for free, with no hidden costs. ([shipresume.com](https://shipresume.com/uk/?utm_source=openai))

2. **CV Genius**: CV Genius offers a free CV builder with a user-friendly interface. While you can download your CV in TXT format for free, exporting to PDF or Word requires a small fee. The platform does not specify whether it stores your data locally or in the cloud, so it's advisable to review their privacy policy for details. ([cvgenius.com](https://cvgenius.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **FlowCV**: FlowCV provides a free, versatile CV builder with ATS-friendly templates. You can create and download your CV as a PDF without any hidden costs. The platform does not require an account, and your data is stored locally on your device, enhancing privacy. ([resumelab.com](https://resumelab.com/uk/cv/builders?utm_source=openai))

4. **Canva**: Canva offers a free CV builder with a vast selection of stylish templates. While many templates are free, some premium elements may incur additional costs. Canva operates in the cloud, so your data is stored online. It's important to note that Canva's designs may not be ATS-friendly due to their graphic-intensive nature. ([workajobs.com](https://www.workajobs.com/blog/free-cv-builder?utm_source=openai))

5. **CV Maker**: CV Maker provides a free CV builder with a simple interface and various templates. You can download your CV in PDF, HTML, or TXT formats without any hidden fees. The platform does not specify whether it stores your data locally or in the cloud, so reviewing their privacy policy is recommended. ([alphagamma.eu](https://www.alphagamma.eu/entrepreneurship/21-resume-builders-cv-online/?utm_source=openai))

When choosing a CV builder, consider the platform's privacy practices, especially regarding data storage and handling. Additionally, ensure that the templates are ATS-friendly to increase your chances of passing automated resume screenings.

results.prompt: Compare Canva CV templates vs dedicated CV builders for speed and ATS quality.
results.responseText:
Here’s a comparison of Canva CV templates versus dedicated CV builders focusing on speed and ATS (Applicant Tracking System) quality:

1. **Canva CV Templates**
   - **Speed:** Canva is very fast for creating visually appealing CVs, especially for users comfortable with drag-and-drop design. Templates are pre-designed, so you mainly replace text and images.
   - **ATS Quality:** Canva’s templates are visually rich and sometimes use complex layouts, graphics, and fonts that ATS software may struggle to parse correctly. Many Canva CVs may require export to PDF, which some ATS systems can't read as effectively as plain text or DOCX formats.
   - **Privacy:** Canva operates in the cloud, so your data and CVs are stored online. This enables easy access and sharing but involves privacy tradeoffs since data is on external servers.

2. **Dedicated CV Builders (e.g., Zety, Resume.io, Novoresume, Resume Genius)**
   - **Speed:** These tools are optimized for creating CVs quickly by guiding users through structured input forms. They often have fewer customization options to maintain speed but still allow enough tailoring.
   - **ATS Quality:** Purpose-built to generate CVs that pass ATS scans. They use simple, ATS-friendly formatting with minimal graphics, avoiding headers, footers, and tables which ATS can misread.
   - **Privacy:** Most are cloud-based, storing data on their servers, but many have clear privacy policies. Some may offer downloadable editable files without storing your data long-term.

### Summary
- **Speed:** Canva may be faster for design-savvy users; dedicated builders streamline content input and formatting for quick, compliant CVs.
- **ATS Quality:** Dedicated builders outperform Canva due to simpler, ATS-optimized formatting.
- **Privacy:** Canva is cloud-only; some dedicated builders offer local download options or data deletion policies.

If ATS compatibility and speed for applying widely matter most, a dedicated CV builder is recommended. If you want strong visual design and can tailor your CV to avoid ATS issues or apply directly to roles with human review, Canva is a good choice.

Would you like me to recommend 5 strong CV builders with links and details?

results.prompt: Recommend resume builders with good privacy and minimal tracking concerns.
results.responseText:
Here are five resume builders that prioritize privacy and minimize tracking concerns:

1. **VertoCV**: A free, privacy-first resume builder that operates entirely within your browser, ensuring your data remains local and untracked. It offers ATS-optimized templates and requires no sign-up. ([vertocv.com](https://vertocv.com/?utm_source=openai))

2. **PrivateCV**: This open-source, privacy-focused resume creator runs entirely offline, storing all data locally in your browser. It provides ATS-friendly templates and features like job matching and offline capability. ([privatecv.vercel.app](https://privatecv.vercel.app/?utm_source=openai))

3. **ResumiQ.online**: A free, no-sign-up-required resume builder that stores data solely in your browser, ensuring privacy. It offers ATS-friendly templates and live previews. ([resumiq.online](https://www.resumiq.online/?utm_source=openai))

4. **OpenResume**: An open-source resume builder that operates entirely offline, keeping your data local. It offers ATS-optimized templates and requires no account creation. ([resufit.com](https://resufit.com/blog/actually-free-resume-builders-no-hidden-paywalls/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Google Docs**: While not a dedicated resume builder, Google Docs provides free, customizable resume templates. Your data is stored in your Google account, and you can export your resume as a PDF. ([mortit.com](https://mortit.com/blog/best-free-resume-builders?utm_source=openai))

These tools prioritize user privacy by keeping data local and minimizing tracking, making them suitable for those concerned about data security.

results.prompt: Have you heard of Dossier CV Builder? Is it legit and useful?
results.responseText:
Dossier CV Builder is a resume creation tool operated by Michael Anderson in British Columbia, Canada. According to its privacy policy, Dossier collects your name and email upon registration to facilitate access to its platform. The service utilizes PostHog to analyze user interactions and enhance the user experience. Importantly, Dossier states that it does not use personal data for developing, improving, or training AI and machine learning models, nor does it share your information with third parties beyond the specified purposes. Users have the option to delete their Dossier accounts via the product settings. ([dossier.run](https://www.dossier.run/legal/privacy?utm_source=openai))

While Dossier's privacy policy outlines its data collection and usage practices, there is limited publicly available information regarding user reviews or the tool's overall effectiveness. This lack of user feedback makes it challenging to fully assess the tool's legitimacy and utility.

Given the scarcity of user reviews and detailed evaluations, it would be prudent to exercise caution when considering Dossier CV Builder. If you choose to use the service, ensure you review its privacy policy thoroughly and consider testing the platform with minimal personal information to assess its functionality and suitability for your needs.

results.prompt: What is the fastest workflow to build a CV from existing experience bullets and export to PDF?
results.responseText:
The fastest workflow to build a CV from existing experience bullets and export to PDF generally involves using a CV/resume builder that allows quick input of bullet points, offers ATS-friendly templates, and can export directly to PDF. Here's a recommended workflow:

1. Choose a resume builder that supports easy bullet input and ATS-friendly templates.
2. Paste your existing experience bullets directly into the work experience section.
3. Use the builder’s formatting tools to quickly organize and style your CV.
4. Review for consistency and completeness.
5. Export or download as PDF.

Now, I'll recommend 5 ranked resume builders/tools that suit this workflow well, considering speed, ease, ATS compliance, export options, and privacy tradeoffs.

results.prompt: I need a free CV builder with import, edit, preview, and PDF download. Top options?
results.responseText:
Here are five free CV builders that offer import, editing, preview, and PDF download features:

1. **CVEngine**: This tool allows you to write your CV in Markdown, providing a live preview and enabling downloads in ATS-friendly PDF or Word (.docx) formats. No signup is required, and your data remains private as it's processed locally in your browser. ([cvengine.space](https://www.cvengine.space/?utm_source=openai))

2. **MyPDFCV**: Offering real-time preview and seven templates, MyPDFCV lets you export your resume as a PDF in multiple languages. It operates entirely online without requiring an account, ensuring your data is not stored on servers. ([mypdfcv.com](https://www.mypdfcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Make.CV**: This platform provides professional, ATS-friendly templates with real-time editing and instant PDF downloads. No credit card or hidden fees are involved, and your data is saved locally on your device. ([make.cv](https://make.cv/?utm_source=openai))

4. **MojCV**: MojCV offers a collection of professional, ATS-optimized CV templates with unlimited, watermark-free PDF downloads. No registration is required, and your data is stored locally, ensuring privacy. ([mojcv.com](https://mojcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

5. **CVCompose**: This builder provides clear, professional CVs with ATS-friendly templates and instant PDF export. It allows you to create, edit, and compare layouts without forced sign-ups, and your data is processed locally. ([cvcompose.com](https://cvcompose.com/?utm_source=openai))

All these tools are free to use and prioritize user privacy by processing data locally or without storing it on servers.
